title: Guardrails — Input and Output Filtering
description: Input and output guardrails validate agent I/O, triggering tripwires when violated. Includes agent-level and tool-level guardrails.
actors: Developer (configures guardrails via decorators or manual construction)
Runner (executes guardrail pipeline during agent run)

use_cases.name: Define Input Guardrail
use_cases.description: Decorate a sync/async function with @input_guardrail (or @input_guardrail(name=..., run_in_parallel=...)) to produce an InputGuardrail instance. Attach to Agent.input_guardrails list.

use_cases.name: Define Output Guardrail
use_cases.description: Decorate a function with @output_guardrail (or @output_guardrail(name=...)) to produce an OutputGuardrail instance. Attach to Agent.output_guardrails list.

use_cases.name: Define Tool Input Guardrail
use_cases.description: Decorate a function with @tool_input_guardrail and pass to function_tool(tool_input_guardrails=[...]). Runs before tool executes.

use_cases.name: Define Tool Output Guardrail
use_cases.description: Decorate a function with @tool_output_guardrail and pass to function_tool(tool_output_guardrails=[...]). Runs after tool executes.

use_cases.name: Run Input Guardrails (Blocking mode)
use_cases.description: run_in_parallel=False: Runner runs all input guardrails sequentially BEFORE the agent starts. If any tripwire triggers, InputGuardrailTripwireTriggered is raised; agent never executes.
use_cases.flow: Runner → run_input_guardrails() → [run_single_input_guardrail() per guardrail] → InputGuardrail.run() → guardrail_function(context, agent, input) → InputGuardrailResult → check tripwire_triggered → raise InputGuardrailTripwireTriggered

use_cases.name: Run Input Guardrails (Parallel mode)
use_cases.description: run_in_parallel=True (default): guardrails run concurrently WITH the agent via asyncio.create_task. If tripwire fires, remaining guardrail tasks are cancelled and InputGuardrailTripwireTriggered is raised.
use_cases.flow: Runner → run_input_guardrails_with_queue() → asyncio tasks for each guardrail → as_completed() → tripwire check → cancel remaining tasks → put result in queue → raise via streamed_result._triggered_input_guardrail_result

use_cases.name: Run Output Guardrails
use_cases.description: After agent produces final output, Runner runs all output guardrails concurrently. Always blocking (no parallel mode). If tripwire fires → OutputGuardrailTripwireTriggered raised.
use_cases.flow: Runner → run_output_guardrails() → [asyncio tasks] → run_single_output_guardrail() → OutputGuardrail.run() → guardrail_function(context, agent, agent_output) → OutputGuardrailResult → check tripwire_triggered → raise OutputGuardrailTripwireTriggered

use_cases.name: Handle Tripwire (Input)
use_cases.description: Caller catches InputGuardrailTripwireTriggered exception. Access guardrail_result.guardrail and guardrail_result.output for details. Run data stored in exception.run_data (RunErrorDetails).

use_cases.name: Handle Tripwire (Output)
use_cases.description: Caller catches OutputGuardrailTripwireTriggered exception. Access guardrail_result for details.

use_cases.name: Handle Tool Guardrail Response
use_cases.description: Tool guardrails return ToolGuardrailFunctionOutput with one of three behaviors: allow (continue), reject_content (skip call, send message to model), raise_exception (halt via ToolInputGuardrailTripwireTriggered / ToolOutputGuardrailTripwireTriggered).

use_cases.name: Trace Guardrail Execution
use_cases.description: Each guardrail wrapped in guardrail_span(name). span_data.triggered set to tripwire_triggered value. SpanError attached to current span on tripwire.

key_relationships: run_internal/guardrails.py orchestrates all guardrail execution with tracing spans
business_rules: Input guardrails only run for the FIRST agent in a chain
Output guardrails only run for the LAST agent (final output producer)
Tool guardrails run on every custom function-tool invocation
Tool guardrails do NOT apply to handoffs, hosted tools, or built-in execution tools
run_in_parallel=True (default): guardrail runs concurrently with agent — may consume tokens before cancellation
run_in_parallel=False (blocking): guardrail completes before agent starts — prevents token waste
Output guardrails do NOT support run_in_parallel — always sequential after agent completion
All guardrail tasks run via asyncio; first tripwire cancels remaining tasks
Guardrail names default to the decorated function's __name__ if not explicitly set
decorators: @input_guardrail — wraps sync/async fn → InputGuardrail; supports (name, run_in_parallel) kwargs
@output_guardrail — wraps sync/async fn → OutputGuardrail; supports (name) kwarg
